answer:
\boxed{D}  
Vitamin A is fat-soluble and stored in the body, so excessive intake can lead to toxicity (hypervitaminosis A). In contrast, vitamin C is water-soluble and excess is usually excreted, while vitamin D and zinc also have upper limits but vitamin A poses a higher risk of overdose from supplements. Therefore, among the options, vitamin A is most likely to be consumed well above safe levels.
